generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "The motor component of the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, driving the cycloidal gearbox for machining applications with ±190° working range and 75°/s maximum angular velocity"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "The cycloidal gearbox component of the robot's first vertical axis, transmitting rotational motion from the motor to the robot's drive train with torque and angular velocity characteristics"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly mounted encoder on the motor shaft measuring motor angle and angular velocity for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngleData, MotorVelocityData, MotorCurrentData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder mounted behind the gearbox measuring gearwheel rotational position and angular velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearwheelAngleData, GearwheelVelocityData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearwheelAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance GearwheelVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

// C5: Data traces
instance MeasuredMotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from motor encoder measuring motor position in degrees"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngleData
]

instance MeasuredMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from motor encoder measuring motor angular velocity in degrees per second"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorVelocityData
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from motor encoder measuring input current (I_M) converted to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-specific motor constants"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
]

instance MeasuredGearwheelAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from secondary encoder measuring gearwheel position in degrees"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearwheelAngleData
]

instance MeasuredGearwheelVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from secondary encoder measuring gearwheel angular velocity in degrees per second"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearwheelVelocityData
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time identification and continuous refinement of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) using live sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossDetectionInsight, ModelValidationInsight
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustments to robot motion accuracy by refining friction models during operation through real-time data comparison"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance OperationalDecisionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard visualization for identifying friction-related deviations and planning maintenance or tool changes"
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenancePlanningAction, ToolChangeAction
]

instance FrictionLossDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of friction losses in the gearbox system"
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of friction models against measured data"
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to improve motion accuracy through friction compensation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenancePlanningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Planning maintenance actions based on detected friction issues"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ToolChangeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to change tools based on operational requirements"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBDDModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram describing structural components (Axis, Motor, Gearbox) and their interconnections (rotational/torque flows) for the first vertical robot axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance SysMLIBDModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram specifying physical composition of the first axis (motor → gearwheel → cycloidal gearbox) with detailed internal connections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance MATLABSimulinkModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink models simulating ideal (frictionless) and friction-affected behaviors with Coulomb-Viscous friction models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction model outputs (τ̇_M, ω̇_M) for comparison with measured data to assess discrepancies between ideal and actual system behavior"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedFrictionData
]

instance SimulatedFrictionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MATLABSimulinkModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling platform used for structural and behavioral modeling (BDD, IBD) of the robot's first vertical axis components"
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLBDDModel, SysMLIBDModel
]

instance MATLABSimulinkToolingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink platform for executable friction models and system simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables MATLABSimulinkModel
]

instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming from robot encoders (motor and secondary) to digital twin system"
]

instance CustomDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard integrating data traces, model outputs, and friction analysis results for operational understanding"
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalDecisionService
]

// C12: Data Transmission Service
instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data aggregation from physical measurements (motor current converted to torque) into structured data traces linked to digital shadow models"
    DTDFVocab:enables MQTTProtocolEnabler
    DTDFVocab:provides MeasuredMotorAngleData, MeasuredMotorVelocityData,
                 MeasuredMotorCurrentData, MeasuredGearwheelAngleData,
                 MeasuredGearwheelVelocityData
]

// C13: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator,
              MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder,
              MeasuredMotorAngleData, MeasuredMotorVelocityData,
              MeasuredMotorCurrentData, MeasuredGearwheelAngleData,
              MeasuredGearwheelVelocityData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and near-real-time**:
   - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., encoders, motor current) is streamed continuously via MQTT, enabling **low-latency processing** (sub-second updates).
   - **Model updates**: Friction models are dynamically refined during operation, with iterative adjustments based on live measurements (e.g., torque/velocity deviations).
   - **Synchronization**: The digital shadow’s processing pipeline (e.g., MathWorks MATLAB Simulink simulations) aligns with operational cycles, ensuring real-time feedback for control adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **single-axis specific** for the first robot axis, but the methodology supports integration with broader systems:
   - **Core Components**:
     - **Digital Shadow (DS)**: Passive real-time representation of the first axis, linking live data (encoders, motor current) to friction models.
     - **Processing Pipeline**: Consumes sensor data and simulation models (e.g., Simulink) to compute friction losses and generate data traces.
   - **Orchestration**: The DS serves as a foundational baseline for future **digital twin** development, enabling hierarchical extension to other axes or subsystems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates across the following lifecycle stages:
   - **As designed**: Idealized frictionless models (e.g., Simulink simulations without friction) serve as baseline comparisons.
   - **As manufactured**: Real-time friction data from operational encoders and motor current are captured and linked to the physical system’s actual behavior.
   - **As operated**: Continuous refinement of friction models during runtime to account for wear, load variations, and environmental factors (e.g., temperature)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Verification**:
     - **Model Validation**: Comparison of simulated friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) with measured torque/velocity data.
     - **Uncertainty Quantification**: Encoder differences reduce positional uncertainty by analyzing elasticity/friction via secondary encoder data.
   - **Validation**:
     - **Real-time Data Alignment**: MQTT streaming ensures low-latency, synchronized data between physical and digital representations.
     - **Traceability**: Metadata links data points to their computational origins (e.g., Simulink models), enabling root-cause analysis of discrepancies."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s technical connection relies on:
   - **Network Protocols**: **MQTT** for real-time sensor data streaming from the robot’s encoders and motor current.
   - **Data Flow**: One-way passive data flow (Digital Shadow Reference Model) from physical sensors to digital models, with active processing components (e.g., Simulink) interpreting data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to:
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Standardized concepts (e.g., Purpose, DataTrace, Model types) from Michael et al. [15].
   - **SysML**: For structural and behavioral modeling (BDD, IBD).
   - **MQTT Protocol**: For real-time data exchange in manufacturing environments."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin addresses safety by:
   - **Data Isolation**: Passive digital shadow avoids autonomous control of the physical system, mitigating unintended actions.
   - **Operational Monitoring**: Continuous friction analysis ensures timely detection of anomalies (e.g., wear, misalignment) to prevent accidents.
   - **Human Oversight**: Dashboard visualization supports operators in interpreting system behavior and making informed decisions."
]
